Issue: javascript: URI used as href — XSS vector; clicking the link executes arbitrary JavaScript in the user browser
Reference: Salesforce Security Implementation Guide — XSS Prevention · OWASP Top 10 A03
Suggestion: Remove the javascript: href entirely. Use a LWC event handler with onclick={handleClick} on a lightning-button instead.

Issue: innerHTML assigned with unsanitized string containing onerror event handler — stored XSS; executes script when image fails to load
Reference: Salesforce LWC Security Guide — innerHTML · OWASP Top 10 A03
Suggestion: Never assign to innerHTML. Use reactive template rendering or DOMParser with explicit sanitization.

Issue: eval() called with a string literal — explicit eval; allows arbitrary code execution
Reference: Salesforce LWC Security Guide — no-eval · OWASP Top 10 A03
Suggestion: Remove eval() entirely. Refactor to use direct function calls or conditional logic.

Issue: document.querySelector used directly — bypasses LWC Locker Service; accesses DOM outside component shadow root
Reference: Salesforce LWC Security Guide — Locker Service
Suggestion: Replace document.querySelector with this.template.querySelector to stay within component scope.

Issue: setTimeout called with a string argument — implied eval; treated as code string by browser engines
Suggestion: Replace with setTimeout(() => { ... }, 10) using a function reference.

Issue: @api property (title) reassigned inside connectedCallback — LWC no-api-reassignments violation; parent-supplied value overwritten
Reference: LWC Developer Guide — @api properties are read-only inside component
Suggestion: Use an internal tracked property (e.g. _title) and initialise from @api title via getter/setter. Never assign to @api directly.

Issue: missing alt attribute — allows onerror-based XSS if src is dynamic; also accessibility violation
Reference: WCAG 2.1 — Alt Text · Salesforce LWC Accessibility Guide
Suggestion: Add a descriptive alt attribute: .

Issue: var used instead of let/const — function-scoped hoisting risk; LWC no-var lint rule
Reference: LWC Developer Guide — Best Practices · ESLint no-var
Suggestion: Replace var unused = 123 with const unused = 123 or remove the unused variable entirely.

Issue: Loose equality (==) used instead of strict equality (===) — type coercion risk; eqeqeq lint rule
console.log calls present in production component — no-console lint rule; leaks debug output to browser console
Suggestion: Remove all console.log statements. Use Platform Event or custom log object if observability is needed.

Issue: Hardcoded external image URL (https://example.com/image.png) not listed in CSP Trusted Sites — browser may block image load
Reference: Salesforce Help — Content Security Policy Overview
Suggestion: Add example.com to Setup > CSP Trusted Sites with img-src directive. Or host the image as a static resource via $Resource.

Issue: doGet() returns userId and orgId to any authenticated REST caller without access-level check — information disclosure risk on shared orgs
Reference: Salesforce Security Implementation Guide — REST Endpoint Security
Suggestion: Add a permission check (e.g. FeatureManagement.checkPermission()) before returning org/user identity data to callers.

Issue: Class mixes three unrelated responsibilities: calcDiscount + classifyAccount + computeRiskScore — violates Single Responsibility Principle
Reference: Clean Code — SRP · Salesforce Well-Architected Framework
Suggestion: Split into DiscountService / AccountClassificationService / RiskScoringService. Each class should have one reason to change.

Issue: classifyAccount() has 7-branch if/else chain on revenue/employee/name — adding a new tier requires modifying the method body
Reference: Salesforce Developer Blog — Metadata Trigger Framework · Apex Enterprise Patterns
Suggestion: Replace with Custom Metadata-driven rules table (ClassificationRule__mdt). Method becomes a loop over rules — open for extension without modification.

Issue: catch(Exception e) swallows exception and substitutes magic number score=1 with no logging — silent failure hides bugs and makes debugging impossible
Suggestion: Log via System.debug(LoggingLevel.ERROR ...) or Platform Event. Document why score=1 is the correct fallback. Consider re-throwing.

Issue: updateAccountDescriptions() has no bulk test with >= 200 records — method calls DML on a List and must be verified at bulk scale
Reference: Apex Testing Best Practices — Bulk Testing
Suggestion: Add a test inserting 200 accounts and calling updateAccountDescriptions() with all 200 IDs. Assert updatedCount == 200.

Issue: pingReturnsOk() assertions are too weak: orgId != null and timestampUtc.contains('T') — does not verify actual org identity or full timestamp format
Reference: Apex Testing Best Practices — Meaningful Assertions
Suggestion: Strengthen to: assertEquals(UserInfo.getOrganizationId(), out.orgId); assertEquals(20, out.timestampUtc.length()); assert(out.timestampUtc.endsWith('Z')).

Issue: getAccountsByType() filters on Account.Type which is not a standard indexed field — monitor selectivity if Account volume exceeds 100K records
Suggestion: If Account grows beyond 100K records consider adding additional WHERE filters or requesting a custom index on Type field.
